rating: 16+ — blood, death, romance, moral complexity. No outcome is protected.
tagline: Where every truth is a lie waiting to be remembered
overview: Arion World is a vast universe spanning multiple timelines, dimensions, and civilizations. At its core lies a fundamental mystery: the Arion Fracture — an event that shattered the fabric of time and scattered fragments of a hidden truth across thousands of years. Characters across different eras unknowingly carry pieces of this truth, and only by connecting the threads across timelines will the full picture emerge.

The Founding Age.description: The age when the original Arion civilisation rose and fell. The first gods walked among mortals. The source of all power — the Luminal Core — was created and then deliberately hidden.
key_events: The Birth of the First Arion (Year 0)
The Great Betrayal — a god kills another god to seize the Core (Year 312)
The Arion Fracture — reality cracks into multiple parallel timelines (Year 400)

The Shadow Centuries.description: The dark age. Civilisations rise and fall without knowing why time feels broken. Ancient orders hunt for the Luminal Core fragments scattered by the Fracture.

The Present Age.description: The primary timeline where the main story unfolds. A young outcast named Kairo discovers he can see echoes of other timelines — visions dismissed as madness by everyone around him.

The Echo Timeline.description: An alternate version of the present where the Great Betrayal was prevented. Here the world developed completely differently — but is slowly being erased as timelines collapse toward a single point.

The Far Future.description: Brief glimpses only. A technologically advanced but spiritually hollow world where the Luminal Core was found and misused. These scenes exist as warnings embedded in the present story.

power_system.name: Luminal Resonance
power_system.description: All living beings carry a trace of the original Luminal Core inside them — called a Resonance Spark. Most people can't access it. Those who can are called Resonants.

tiers.name: Echo Sight
tiers.description: Can perceive emotional residue left on objects and places

tiers.name: Thread Weaving
tiers.description: Can manipulate minor aspects of reality — light, gravity, time perception

tiers.name: Fracture Walking
tiers.description: Can briefly step between timelines — extremely rare, considered mythical

tiers.name: Core Binding
tiers.description: Theoretical only — what happens when someone reunites all Core fragments. Unknown consequences.

power_system.hidden_truth: Every use of Luminal Resonance slowly erases the user from all timelines. The more power used, the more 'real' a person becomes in one timeline and ghost-like in others. This explains why many Resonants eventually vanish — not death, but temporal erasure.

The Veilkeepers.role: Ancient secret society that knows the truth about the Arion Fracture. Their mission: prevent the Luminal Core from being reassembled at all costs.
The Veilkeepers.secret: They caused the Fracture deliberately to trap a god inside it

The Resonant Guild.role: Organisation that trains and regulates Resonants. Appears benevolent.
The Resonant Guild.secret: Upper leadership are Veilkeeper puppets who harvest Resonance energy from students

The Shattered.role: People who partially lost their timeline anchor — they exist partly in multiple timelines simultaneously, appearing to others as unstable or insane.
The Shattered.secret: They are the only ones who can naturally perceive the full truth of the Arion Fracture

The Unnamed Court.role: Unknown. References appear in ancient texts only. No confirmed members.

Caelum City.description: The sprawling capital of the present age. Built atop the ruins of an ancient Arion city — but no one knows it.
Caelum City.hidden_truth: The city's foundation stones are fragments of the original Luminal Core structure

The Fractured Wastes.description: A vast desert where time moves strangely — clocks stop, people age in reverse, echoes of past events play on repeat
The Fractured Wastes.hidden_truth: The geographic centre of the original Arion Fracture event

The Undercity.description: Ancient tunnels beneath Caelum City. Officially 'sealed for safety'.
The Undercity.hidden_truth: Contains a fully intact Founding Age archive — and a door that leads to the Echo Timeline

active_mysteries: Who really caused the Arion Fracture and why?
Why can Kairo see other timelines when no Resonant has ever done this?
What happened to the original Arion people — did they die or transform into something else?
Why does the god trapped in the Fracture sometimes speak through random people?
What is written on the door in the Undercity that no one can read?
